---
title: "Analysis of Class Data"
author: "Elijah Kearl"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
        source: false
        toggle: true
        caption: See code
execute: 
  warning: false
    
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd 
import numpy as np
import sqlite3
from lets_plot import *

LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)
```


```{python}

buildings = [
    "ASC", "Ag Engineering Bldg", "Austin", "BEN", "Benson",
    "Clarke", "Court", "ETC", "Hart", "Hinckley", "MC", "Ricks",
    "Romney", "STC", "Smith", "Snow", "Spori", "Taylor",
    "Taylor Chapel", "University Comm Bldg",
]

df = pd.read_sql_query("SELECT * FROM courses", sqlite3.connect("parsed.db"))

df.loc[df.schedule_building.str.contains("Arranged", na=False), "schedule_building"] = np.nan

df['schedule_building'] = df['schedule_building'].replace('NULL', np.nan)

df.dropna(subset=['schedule_building'], inplace=True)

for b in buildings:
    mask = df.schedule_building.str.contains(b, na=False)
    df.loc[mask, "schedule_building"] = b

df.to_sql("courses", sqlite3.connect("cleaned.db"), if_exists="replace", index=False)

```




## QUESTION 1

__What is the best month to fly if you want to avoid delays of any length?__ 

```{python}
conn = sqlite3.connect("cleaned.db")


query = """
SELECT *
FROM courses;
"""

table = pd.read_sql_query(query, conn)


print(table.schedule_time_range[0])

# plot (or inspect) the results
# ggplot(delay_month, aes(x='schedule', y='course_section', fill='schedule')) + \
#     geom_bar(stat='identity') + \
#     labs(x="Schedule", y="Number of courses", title="Number of courses by schedule")

```
